heading: Procurement and sale of stores to members and civilian employees
label: 2709 Procurement and sale of stores to members and civilian employees
text: Such stores as the Secretary may designate may be procured and sold to members of the Coast Guard, and to the surviving spouses of such members. Such designated stores may also be procured and sold to civilian officers and employees of the United States, and to such other individuals as may be specifically authorized by the Secretary, at Coast Guard stations and other units beyond the continental limits of the United States or in Alaska.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesDerived from title 34, U.S.C., 1946 ed., § 533 (, ; , ; , ; , ; , ).Mar. 3, 1909, ch. 25535 Stat. 768Apr. 14, 1937, ch. 7850 Stat. 63June 10, 1939, ch. 19653 Stat. 814Jan. 23, 1942, ch. 1556 Stat. 13Apr. 9, 1943, ch. 3957 Stat. 60Said section granted authority for the Secretary of the Navy to sell designated stores to officers and enlisted men. It is deemed desirable to grant similar authority to the Secretary having control of the Coast Guard.Said section would in no way be affected. 81st Congress, House Report No. 557.

Amendments2021— substituted “individuals” for “persons”.Pub. L. 116–2832018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–282section 487 of this title1984— in section catchline substituted “members” for “officers, enlisted men,” and in text substituted “surviving spouses” for “widows” and “members” for “officers and enlisted men” in two places.Pub. L. 98–557
